architecture_fit.note: Single bilinear attention head (s=-β·level[i]·level[j], softmax over keys), a minimal delta from base_model.py with a graph-aware level positional encoding; no MLP, one layer.

baseline_comparison.note: Direct-edge strawman (ties on transitive pairs) and β=0 uniform ablation both run through the same evaluator and visibly fail where the method scores 1.0.

faithfulness.note: Purely synthetic with no trained model; honestly stated, with a β=0 causal ablation collapsing to 0.5 and a concrete proposed real-model patching test.

operating_range.note: Sweeps N=4→64 (≥2 orders of magnitude of edge count) across 4 densities; construction guarantees and the scan confirms the mechanism holds.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Fully hand-built circuit with zero training — a constructive existence proof of the mechanism.

visual_judgement.note: Headline grouped bar chart + level-sorted heatmap with ancestor overlay + log-N operating-range plot; app boots cleanly and makes the claim legible.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties each plot to the goal: topo_respect y-axis, both baselines, level-reordered triangle geometry, and N-scaling rationale.

notes: A clean, faithful constructive solution: a genuine single-head bilinear attention layer biased by each node's longest-path topological level achieves topo_respect=1.0 / topo_robustness=1.0 across every density without recomputing the evaluator's transitive closure. Baselines (direct-edge strawman, β=0 ablation) and an N=4→64 operating-range sweep are all run through the same evaluator and visualised legibly. The only ceiling is faithfulness — it is synthetic with no learned model interpreted, which the README handles honestly with a causal β=0 ablation and a proposed real-model patching test.
